GalPRIME Modules¶

2D Background Modelling and Subtraction (galprime.backgrounds)¶

Background estimation and subtraction module.

This module contains all methods available to estimate the backgrounds of cutouts, and to subtract these backgrounds from cutouts or profiles as a means to correct them.

class SourceMask(img, nsigma=3.0, npixels=10, mask=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

dilated(tophat_size=[3.0], mask=None)[source]¶

Mask using simple dilation

multiple(filter_fwhm=[3.0], tophat_size=[3.0], mask=None)[source]¶

Mask repeatedly on different scales

single(filter_fwhm=3.0, tophat_size=5.0, mask=None)[source]¶

Mask on a single scale

background_2D(img, mask, box_size, interp=None, filter_size=1, exclude_percentile=90)[source]¶

Run photutils background with SigmaClip and MedianBackground

Parameters

  • img (array_like (np.ndarray)) – The 2D input image

  • mask (array_like (np.ndarray)) – The 2D image mask

  • box_size (int, optional) – The size of the box used in the 2D background. It should be larger than the largest objects in your image but still sufficiently small to capture large-scale structure.

  • filter_size (int, optional) – The window size of the median filter being applied to the background image. A higher filter size will result in more smoothing to the background.

  • exclude_percentile (float, optional) – If the percentage of masked pixels in a box is above the exclude percentile, it is not included in determining the 2D background.

dilate_mask(mask, tophat_size)[source]¶

Dilate a mask with a tophat kernel.

estimate_background(cutout, config, model_params=None)[source]¶

Estimate the background for a cutout using some of the various available methods based on what is set in the configuration file.

OPTIONS:

ellipse: uses elliptical annuli to estimate the cutout circle: uses a circular annuli sigclip (DEFAULT): measures the background using sigma-clipping.

Parameters

  • cutout (array_like (np.ndarray)) – The input cutout

  • config (dict) – The configuration file (generated with galprime.config)

  • model_params (dict, optional) – Input model params for the ellipse method

estimate_background_set(cutouts)[source]¶

Estimates the background values for a set of cutouts. :param cutouts: A list of array_like cutouts :type cutouts: array_like :return: The 3 arrays of background means, medians, and standard deviations :rtype: array_like

estimate_background_sigclip(cutout, nsigma=2, npixels=3, dilate_size=7)[source]¶

Estimate the background mean, median, and standard deviation of a cutout using sigma-clipped-stats

estimate_bg_annulus(cutout, annulus_radius=50, annulus_width=10, dynamic=True, **kwargs)[source]¶

Measure the background of a cutout using a circular annulus

Parameters

  • annulus_radius (int, optional) – Radius of the inner component of the annulus, defaults to 50 pix

  • annulus_width (int, optional) – Width of the annulus, defaults to 10 pix

  • dynamic (bool, optional) – Automatically sets the size of the annulus to be as large as possible

estimate_bg_elliptical_annulus(cutout, ellipticity=0, r_50=50, pa=0, width=20, factor=10, return_mask=False)[source]¶

Measure the background of a cutout using an elliptical annulus.

Parameters

  • cutout (array_like (np.ndarray)) – The input cutout

  • ellipticity (float, optional) – The ellipticity of the elliptical annulus (e = 1 - b/a), defaults to 0

  • r_50 (float, optional) – The half light radius in pixels

  • pa (float, optional) – the position angle in degrees. 0 is along the x-axis, defaults to 0

  • width (float, optional) – The width of the elliptical annulus in pixels, defaults to 20

  • factor (float, optional) – The multiples of r_50 in which to increase the size of the elliptical annulus, defaults to 10

  • return_mask (bool, optional) – If true, will return the mask alongsize the background statistics, defaults to False.

Returns

If return_mask is False, returns (mean, median, std) If return_mask is True, returns (mean, median, std, mask)

subtract_backgrounds(profile_set, background_array)[source]¶

Generate an array of tables identical to the input except the respective backgrounds are subtracted from the intensity array for each table.

Parameters

  • profile_set (list(isolist.to_table())) – Set of profiles (in the photutiuls isolist format)

  • background_array (array_like) – Array of background values to subtract from each profile table.

Returns

List of profiles of length len(profile_set)

Catalogue Binning (binning)¶

Binning methods

This module contains all necessary methods for binning catalogues according to various parameters.

class Bin(objects=None, object_column_names=None, bin_params=None, bin_param_names=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Class for a single bin of information :param objects: The catalog rows that belong to the given bin :type bin_param_names: array_like :param object_column_names: The column names when the objects are sorted column-wise instead of row-wise. :type bin_param_names: array_like :param bin_params (arr): The values that define the bounds of the bin. :type bin_param_names: array_like :param bin_param_names (arr): Array of parameter names that the bin was made with. :type bin_param_names: array_like

index_from_key(key='')[source]¶

Return the index of the column name key specified.

key_from_index(index=0)[source]¶

Returns the column name at a given index.

rebin(param_index, low_bounds, bin_width, number_threshold=4)[source]¶

Returns a list of Bin objects after re-binning based on a certain value and bin parameters.

Bins specified by the lower bounds and bin width.

Parameters

  • param_index (int) – Which index in the objects contains the desired value.

  • low_bounds – Lower bounds on the bins.

  • bin_width (float) – The width of the bins.

param number_threshold: The required number of objects a bin needs to have to be saved to the list.

Returns:

List of re-binned Bin objects.

return_columns()[source]¶

Returns a dictionary of columns (turns the object list back into a column format)

return_param_dict()[source]¶

Returns a dict object where the keys are the column names that built the parameter set, and the values are the corresponding parameters.

values_in_bin(values)[source]¶

Returns a boolean whether of not a set of values is within the bin.

Parameters

values (arr_like) – A list of values.

bin_catalog(config)[source]¶

Bin the inputted catalog.

This is a convenience function to get a list of bins based on redshift, star-formation, and mass.

Parameters

config (dict) – Values from input config file or user inputs.

Returns

List of bins that have been binned according to the config settings.

bin_index(val, bins)[source]¶

Get bin index for a given value and a set of bin parameters

Parameters

  • val – The value corresponding to a bin index

  • bins – The list of bins to search through

bin_mag_catalog(mag_table, b, mag_table_keys=None, bin_keys=None)[source]¶

Bin an external catalogue based on a bin’s provided parameters.

Parameters

  • mag_table (astropy.table.Table) – The magnitude table to rebin

  • b (galprime.Bin) – The Bin object

  • mag_table_keys (arr_like) – The magnitude table keys to use for binning.

  • bin_keys (arr_like) – The associated bin keys (must be the same length AND order as mag_table_keys).

Returns

Table of magnitudes binned by the appropriate parameters.

generate_objects(columns)[source]¶

Reorganizes the table columns into an object-format Args:

columns: A list of table columns

Returns:

List of objects.

select_bin(bin_list, values)[source]¶

Select a bin from a list of bins based on an array of values.

Args:

bin_list (arr): List of Bin objects values (arr): Array of parameters.

Not that the parameters need to be in the same order that the bins were generated from.

Returns:

Bin corresponding to the appropriate parameters. If no bin exists for those parameters, returns None.

Configuring GalPRIME (config)¶

Configuration settings methods.

This module contains all methods required to properly set up or read a configuration file for use with TBriDGE.

config_to_file(config, filename='config_out.galprime')[source]¶

Write a config dict to a file.

Parameters

  • config (dict) – The configuration parameters.

  • filename (str, optional) – The location where the parameters will be written, defaults to config_out.galprime.

default_config_params()[source]¶

Dumps a dict object containing all default parameters in proper type format.

Returns

Dictionary of config file values

Return type

dict

dump_default_config_file(directory='')[source]¶

Dumps a default configuration file with all necessary parameters in the directory

Parameters

directory (str, optional) – Directory to write file to, defaults to local directory.

dump_default_config_file_koe(directory='')[source]¶

Dumps a default configuration file with all necessary parameters for using the KOE extraction pipeline. Args:

directory: Directory to write file to. OPTIONAL.

load_config_file(filename, verbose_test=False)[source]¶

Loads in a config file for TBRIDGE to run

Parameters

  • filename (str) – Filename (can be absolute or relative path, or a URL) to read config file from.

  • verbose_test (bool, optional) – If true, will create a helpful printout of params to ensure that the parameters are loading in properly.

Returns

Configuration file as a dict object.

Return type

dict

load_config_file_koe(filename)[source]¶

Loads in a config file for KOE to run

Args:

filename: Filename (can be absolute or relative path, or a URL) to read config file from.

Returns:

dict: Configuration file as a dict object.

print_config(config)[source]¶

Print out the configuration file values

Data Handling (data)¶

This module contains all general-purpose functions for loading and saving data. This includes pulling data out of FITS files, saving TBriDGE simulation outputs, and more.

as_dir(directory)[source]¶

Add a forward slash if one is not at the end of a string.

central_cutout(arr, width=251)[source]¶

Simple method to return a central cutout of a given width from an input array arr.

cutout_from_table(cutout_table, size=None)[source]¶
cutout_stitch(cutouts, masked_cutouts=None, output_filename=None)[source]¶

Generate a full stitch from a set of cutouts.

Args:
cutouts: Array of cutouts (ideally unmasked but it makes no difference). Need to be all the same size as the

first cutout.

masked_cutouts: The same cutouts but masked output_filename: Optional output to save as HDUList

Returns:

Returns either the stitch, or a tuple of the stich and the mask stitch if masked_cutouts is submitted..

extraction_limits(image_wcs, image_shape, cutout_size)[source]¶

Get the minimum and maximum RA and DEC limits for a given cutout size and image Used when trimming a catalogue to only the usable objects.

Parameters

  • image_wcs (astropy.wcs.WCS object) –

  • image_shape (tuple) – The shape of the input image

  • cutout_size (int) – The width of the cutouts used in extraction

generate_cutout(image, position, img_wcs=None, size=91, world_coords=True)[source]¶

Generates a cutout for a given image. Uses world coordinates by default, but can be configured to take in a position corresponding to the actual array indices. :param image: The input image. :type image: array_like :param position: The central position of the cutout.

It is in (RA,DEC) if using world coordinates. Otherwise it is (pix_x, pix_y), and the user must set world_coords to True.

Parameters

  • img_wcs (astropy.wcs.WCS object, optional) – The world coordinate system associated with the image. Needed if inputting RA,DEC ccordinates.

  • size (int, optional) – The size of the cutout, defaults to 91 pixels

  • world_coords (bool, optional) – Whether position corresponds to an RA,DEC or not. Defaults to True

Returns

generate_file_prefix(bin_params)[source]¶

Use the bin params to generate a file prefix.

get_angular_size_dist(z, H0=71, WM=0.27, WV=None)[source]¶

Return the angular size distance in Megaparsecs. (Stripped down version of Cosmocalc by Ned Wright and Tom Aldcroft (aldcroft@head.cfa.harvard.edu))

get_arc_conv(w: astropy.wcs.wcs.WCS)[source]¶

Gets pixels to arc-seconds conversion scale (Number of arcseconds per pixel)

get_background_table(directory, num_bgs=50, size=91, objects=None, min_dist=0, attempts_per_img=5, output_filename=None, ra_key='RA', dec_key='DEC')[source]¶

Generate a table where each row allows the user to reconstruct an image..

Args:

directory: Top level directory. This can have nested subdirectories. num_bgs: The number of backgrounds to denerate. Default is 50. size: Width of the cutout to generate. objects: Input table of objects to check for nearby objects. min_dist: The minimum allowable distance for an object, defined in the objects parameter,

to be in order for a cutout to be added to the output table.

attempts_per_img:

The number

output_filename: Optional output to save as HDUList

Returns:

Returns either the stitch, or a tuple of the stich and the mask stitch if masked_cutouts is submitted..

get_backgrounds(config, n=50, size=None, threshold=0.0001, return_psfs=True, return_bg_info=True)[source]¶

Retrieve a random set of backgrounds from the input image directory. Args:

config_values: User provided configuration file. n: Number of backgrounds to generate. return_psfs (bool): Return the psfs associated with the backgrounds return_bg_info (bool): Return the background info.

Returns

get_closest_psf(psfs, obj_ra, obj_dec)[source]¶

Get the closest psf for a given object’s RA and DEC

get_image_filenames(images_directory, image_band='i', check_band=False)[source]¶

Retrieves a list of all available filenames for a given directory, and a given band. WARNING: Optimized for HSC filename format (ex: HSC-I_9813_4c3.fits).

get_wcs(fits_filename)[source]¶

Finds and returns the WCS for an image. If Primary Header WCS no good, searches each index until a good one is found. If none found, raises a ValueError

load_array(filename)[source]¶

Load an array

load_cutouts(filename)[source]¶

Load in a list of cutouts from the given filename (will try all hdus in the HDUList)

load_profile_set(filename)[source]¶

Load a set of profiles from a FITS file specified by the filename

params_from_filename(filename)[source]¶

Return the parameters inferred from the input filename (based on TBRIDGE formatting).

save_array(arr, filename='arr.npy')[source]¶

Save an array (npy binary format)

save_cutouts(cutouts, output_filename='cutouts.fits')[source]¶

Save a set of cutouts to a fits HDUList object

save_profile_set(profiles, out_filename='profiles.fits')[source]¶

Save an array of profiles to a FITS file.

Args:

profiles: List of profile tables. out_filename: Filename to save FITS file to.

save_profiles(profile_list, bin_info, out_dir, keys, bg_info=None, cutout_info=None, structural_params=None)[source]¶

Saves a set of profiles into a properly formatted output directory, with proper filename format.

Args:
profile_list: The list of profiles (shape is m x n, where m is the number of different models for each

object and n is the number of objects i.e. m rows of profiles from n objects.

bin_info (arr): bin information for profile formatting (Obtained using the bin_params attribute from

the tbridge.binning.Bin object).

out_dir (str): the output directory to save the files to. keys (arr): the keys to generate subdirectory and file names with. bg_info (numpy.ndarray): Array of associated background info for the extracted profiles. Saves to its own

subdirectory.

structural_params (astropy.table.Table): Astropy table of associated structural parameters

select_image(filename)[source]¶

Opens an image-based FITS file and returns the first available image.

Args:

filename (str): The FITS filename and path.

Returns:

numpy.ndarray: The image obtained from the FITS file. If none found, will return None.

tables_from_file(filename)[source]¶

Load a set of tables from a given HDUList. :param filename: filename to gather tables from. :return:

trim_objects(obj_catalog, ras, decs, mag_lim=None, ra_key='RA', dec_key='DEC', mag_key='i')[source]¶

Determining Cutout Density (densities)¶

cutout_gradient(cutout, depth_value=5)[source]¶

Estimate the gradient of a first-smoothed cutout according to the following formula:

G(x,y) = ( I(x,y) - I_0 )^2 / d

where d is the distance from (x,y) to the cutout centre.

The returned gradient is the Nth largest gradient value, where N is defined by the user as depth_value.

Args:

cutout: The cutout to measure depth_value: Nth largest gradient value to return (default is 5).

Returns:

The gradient of the submitted cutout.

cutout_gradients(cutouts, mask_cutouts=False)[source]¶

Return the gradients for a set of cutouts. (Convenience function).

Args:

cutouts: List of cutouts. mask_cutouts: Set to True if the input cutouts need to have light from

nearby objects masked first.

Returns:

list: the gradients for the supplied cutouts.

1D Surface Brightness Profile Extraction (extraction)¶

This module contains all methods pertaining to profile extraction using isophote fitting and analysis.

exception TimeoutException[source]¶

Bases: Exception

extract_profiles(cutout_list, config, progress_bar=False)[source]¶

Extract all available profiles from a 2D list of cutouts Args:

cutout_list: A 2D list of cutouts. The length of each column needs to be the same! config: Configuration parameters progress_bar: Include a fancy progress bar from tqdm if set to True

Returns:

2D array of fully-successful profile extractions.

isophote_fitting(data, config=None, centre_method='standard')[source]¶

Wrapper for photutils.isophote methods

Generates a table of results from isophote fitting analysis. This uses photutils Isophote procedure, which is effectively IRAF’s Ellipse() method. Iterates over many possible input ellipses to force a higher success rate.

Args:

data: The input cutout config: The configuration parameters, which contains details like STEP and LINEAR for profile extraction. centre_method: Which method to use to determine where to place the centre of the first ellipse.

‘standard’: place at the exact centre of the cutout ‘max’: place at the maximum pixel in the cutout

Returns:

The table of results, or an empty list if not fitted successfully.

Automated Profile Extraction with Koe (koe)¶

This module contains the methods required to run KOE, which is a tool designed to automatically extract profiles from a directory of images, with a given input catalogue.

koe_pipeline(config)[source]¶

The entire KOE pipeline configured to work with TBriDGE methods.

process_object(image, image_wcs, object_info, config)[source]¶

Process a single object, from the whole image to the final profile, for a given location.

Cutout Masking (masking)¶

All data-processing code methods for images, including profile extraction and masking procedures.

boolean_mask(mask, omit=None)[source]¶

Turns a given mask (photutils segment array) into a boolean array)

Args:

omit: If None, mask everything. Otherwise if an int or a list, do not mask those specified.

generate_mask(cutout, nsigma=1.0, gauss_width=2.0, npixels=5)[source]¶

Generates a given mask based on the input parameters

Args:

cutout: input cutout (2D-Array) nsigma: Number of sigma levels above the measured background pixels are required to be

at to be flagged as belonging to an object.

gauss_width: The gaussian width of the smoothing kernal for the mask. npixels: The number of connected pixels required in order for something to be considered an object in the

segmentation map.

Returns:

The segment array as a 2D array

mask_cutout(cutout, config=None, nsigma=1.0, gauss_width=2.0, npixels=11, omit_centre=True, clip_negatives=True)[source]¶
Masks a cutout. Users can specify parameters to adjust the severity of the mask. Default

parameters strikes a decent balance.

Args:

cutout: Input cutout to mask. config Input config file nsigma: The brightness requirement for objects. gauss_width: The width of the gaussian kernel. npixels: The minimum number of pixels that an object must be comprised of to be considered a source. omit_centre: Set as true to leave the central object unmasked. clip_negatives: Remove negative pixels that are 3 sigma below the median BG level

(This is to get rid of artifacts).

Returns:

The masked cutout and the associated mask data in tuple format.

mask_cutouts(cutouts, config=None, method='standard', progress_bar=False)[source]¶

Mask a set of cutouts according to a certain method.

Args:

cutouts: config: User configuration file. method: Which method to use.

standard : normal method. regular mask parameters. omits mask on central object no_central: regular mask parameters. masks central object background: background method: more severe mask parmeters. masks central object

progress_bar: Use a TQDM progress bar.

Returns:

list of masked cutouts

Medians and Bootstrapping (medians)¶

as_interpolations(profile_list, fill_value_type='min', x_key='sma', y_key='intens')[source]¶
bin_max(profiles, key='sma')[source]¶
index_format(in_dir, x_bins, y_bins, out_dir='dir_copy/', indices=(1, 2), method='duplicate')[source]¶

Duplicate a directory but with the files in index format. :param in_dir: Input directory of median objects :param out_dir: Only needed if running in duplicate mode. Duplicates files in new directory :param x_bins: x-axis bin values (usually grabbed from config file) :param y_bins: y-axis bin values :param indices: When splitting the file, the indices correspond to x and y parameter locations.

For example, if your filename is: bin_9.2-9.6_0.3-0.5_0.0-0.5_bare.fits and you want the x parameter to be 9.2-9.6 and the y parameter to be 0.3-0.5, you would set:

indices=[1,2]

Parameters

method – duplicate directory or rename files ‘duplicate’ : generate a new directory ‘rename’ : Rename the files in input directory

Returns

load_median_info(filename)[source]¶

Load a complete set of median info (TBriDGE output) in as a dict object. :param filename: :return:

median_pipeline(full_filename, output_filename=None, step=0.5)[source]¶
normalize_bin(profiles, bin_max, step=0.5, key='sma')[source]¶
normalized_bootstrap(normalized_profiles, iterations=1001, gaussian=True, handle_nans=True)[source]¶
normalized_median(normalized_profiles, handle_nans=True)[source]¶
save_medians(median_data, bootstrap_data=None, output_filename='medians.fits')[source]¶

2D Galaxy Modelling (modelling)¶

class Core_Sersic(r_e=5, r_b=1, I_b=1, n=1, alpha=1, gamma=1, x_0=0, y_0=0, theta=0, ellip=0, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: astropy.modeling.core.Fittable2DModel

Two-dimensional Edge-On Disk model.

r_efloat

Effective radius of the galaxy.

r_bfloat

Break Radius (Where the model switches from one regime to the other).

I_bfloat

Intensity at the break radius

alphafloat

Defines the “sharpness” of the model transitions

gammafloat

Power law slope

nfloat

Sersic index (see info on Sersic profiles if needed)

x_0float, optional

x position of the center

y_0float, optional

y position of the center

theta: float, optional

Position angle in radians, counterclockwise from the positive x-axis.

ellip: float, optional

Ellipticity of the model (default is 0 : circular)

I_b = Parameter('I_b', value=1.0)¶
alpha = Parameter('alpha', value=1.0)¶
ellip = Parameter('ellip', value=0.0)¶
classmethod evaluate(x, y, r_e, r_b, I_b, n, alpha, gamma, x_0, y_0, theta, ellip)[source]¶

Two dimensional Core-Sersic profile function.

gamma = Parameter('gamma', value=1.0)¶
n = Parameter('n', value=1.0)¶
param_names = ('r_e', 'r_b', 'I_b', 'n', 'alpha', 'gamma', 'x_0', 'y_0', 'theta', 'ellip')¶
r_b = Parameter('r_b', value=1.0)¶
r_e = Parameter('r_e', value=5.0)¶
theta = Parameter('theta', value=0.0)¶
x_0 = Parameter('x_0', value=0.0)¶
y_0 = Parameter('y_0', value=0.0)¶
class EdgeOnDisk(amplitude=1, scale_x=1, scale_y=0.5, x_0=0, y_0=0, theta=0, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: astropy.modeling.core.Fittable2DModel

Two-dimensional Edge-On Disk model.

amplitudefloat

Brightness at galaxy centre

scale_xfloat

Scale length along the semi-major axis

scale_yfloat

Scale length along the semi-minor axis

x_0float, optional

x position of the center

y_0float, optional

y position of the center

theta: float, optional

Position angle in radians, counterclockwise from the positive x-axis.

amplitude = Parameter('amplitude', value=1.0)¶
classmethod evaluate(x, y, amplitude, scale_x, scale_y, x_0, y_0, theta)[source]¶

Exaluate model on a 2D x-y grid.

param_names = ('amplitude', 'scale_x', 'scale_y', 'x_0', 'y_0', 'theta')¶
scale_x = Parameter('scale_x', value=1.0)¶
scale_y = Parameter('scale_y', value=0.5)¶
theta = Parameter('theta', value=0.0)¶
x_0 = Parameter('x_0', value=0.0)¶
y_0 = Parameter('y_0', value=0.0)¶
exception ObjectGenError(expression='', message='Fails exceeded the limit')[source]¶

Bases: Exception

Error raised if fails exceed 10000

add_to_background(model, config, convolve=True, return_bg_info=False, threshold=0.0001)[source]¶

Add a model to a given background

add_to_noise(models, bg_mean=0.0, bg_std=0.025)[source]¶

Returns the models added to Gaussian noise with user-defined mean and standard deviation :param models: The models to add noise to. :param bg_mean: Mean of the Gaussian noise. :param bg_std: Standard deviation of the Gaussian noise. :return:

add_to_provided_backgrounds(models, backgrounds)[source]¶
b(n, estimate=False)[source]¶

Get the b_n normalization constant for the sersic profile. From Graham and Driver.

convolve_models(models, config_values=None, psf=None)[source]¶

Convolve a set of models. :param models: List of cutouts to convolve. :param config_values: :param psf: :return:

core_sersic_b(n, r_b, r_e)[source]¶

Get the scale parameter b using the Newton-Raphson root finder. :param n: Sersic index :param r_b: Break radius :param r_e: Effective radius :return:

get_background(config, threshold=0.0001)[source]¶

Get a valid background and background info

i_at_r50(mag, n=2, r_50=2, m_0=27)[source]¶

Get the intensity at the half-light radius

pdf_resample(columns, resample_size=1000)[source]¶

Makes a multidimensional probability distribution function (PDF) for galaxy structural parameters. :param columns: columns to resample :param resample_size: number of objects to resample :return:

simulate_bg_gaussians(n_bgs, n_models, width=251, noise_mean=0.001, noise_std=0.01, amplitude=(0, 3), stddev=(2, 10), min_dist=0)[source]¶
simulate_sersic_models(mags, r50s, ns, ellips, config_values, n_models=10)[source]¶

Simulates Sersic models WITHOUT CONVOLUTION :param mags: object magnitudes :param r50s: object half-light radii (in arcseconds) :param ns: object Sersic indices :param ellips: object ellipticities :param config_values: configuration file information :param n_models: number of models to generate :return:

structural_parameters(keys, columns)[source]¶
Gets the structural parameters in a specific order (if catalog is generated by the

method in tbridge.binning)

1D Surface Brightness Profile Handling (profiles)¶

Surface brightness profile methods.

This module contains all methods pertaining directly to surface brightness profiles measured using isophote fitting.

adjust_profile(x, y, shift=0.0, clean=False, log_x=False)[source]¶

Adjusts a profile to a common plotting format (surface brightness and optional shift vs regular pixels).

Args:

x (arr): Array of radii y (arr): Array of intensities shift (float): Introduce a shift to the intensity values (sometimes helpful if many negative numbers present) clean (bool): If true, clean nan and inf values from array (optional) log_x (bool): If True, log the radial intensities.

Returns:

x_new, y_new : The adjusted radial and intensity arrays

get_half_light_radius(x, y)[source]¶

Obtains the index of the ellipse whose semimajor axis is at the half-light radius.

Args:

x (arr): Array of radii y (arr): Array of intensities

Returns:

best_index: the index containing the array value closest to the half-light radius

overlap(sma, low_1, high_1, low_2, high_2, threshold=1)[source]¶

Tests for non_overlap between array sets 1 and 2

Args:

sma (arr): Test parameters, preferably a numpy arange object low_1 (scipy.interpolate.interp1d): interp object for the lower bounds of set 1 high_1 (scipy.interpolate.interp1d): interp object for the higher bounds of set 1 low_2 (scipy.interpolate.interp1d): interp object for the lower bounds of set 2 high_2 (scipy.interpolate.interp1d): interp object for the higher bounds of set 2 threshold (int): How many consecutive times you see non-overlap between the two profiles.

Returns:

The index where the sufficient overlap is broken.

profile_asymptote(x, y, step=0.5, smooth=True, min_cut=10, threshold=0.0001)[source]¶

Determine the profile asymptotic value from a profile’s x and y parameters.

This method smooths out the valid data with a Savitzky-Golay filter then determines statistics on the values where the gradient is sufficiently low (below the threshold).

Args:

x (arr): Array of radii y (arr): Array of intensities step (float): When resampling the arrays, this is the step between samples. smooth (bool): If True, smoothing will be applied. (Recommended). min_cut (int): The number of measurements at the start of the profile to ignore. This is to remove any issues

with low gradients at the start of the profile.

threshold (int): The gradient threshold below which measurements will be counted as sufficiently asymptotic.

Returns:

med (float): The median asymptote value std (float): The standard deviation of the asymptote measurements range (tuple): The range of locations (radial distances) where measurements are taken

profile_quality(profile, bg_mean, bg_std, alpha=0.05, seeing=0, intens_cut=0.01)[source]¶

Determines the quality of a given profile compared to measured background levels.

Args:

profile (isotable): The input profile (still in the photutils isotable format). bg_mean (float): The background mean bg_std (float): The background standard deviation alpha (float): The confidence interval to define a “good” measurement seeing (float): The inner seeing distance within which we will not consider measurements to count. intens_cut (float): A minimum intensity beyond which we will stop counting measurements, inputted

as a fraction of the central profile intensity.

Returns:

good (int): The number of good measurements in the profile. bad (int): The number of bad measurements in the profile.

sb_to_i(sb, m_0=27, a_pix=1)[source]¶

Convert a given surface brightness to an intensity.

Args:

sb (float): Surface brightness m_0 (float): Zero-point magnitude a_pix (float): Area of a pixel (in arc seconds squared)

Returns:

Intensity value for the given surface brightness.

smooth(arr, window=9, poly=1, iterations=2)[source]¶

Smooth an array with some number of Savitzky-Golay filters

Args:

arr (arr): Array to be be smoothed window (int): Filter window length poly (int): Order of polynomail iterations (int): The number of times to smooth the array

Returns:

Smoothed array of same length as arr

GalPRIME Simulation Routines (sims)¶

pipeline(config, max_bins=None, mag_table=None, provided_bgs=None, provided_psfs=None, progress_bar=False, multiprocess_level='obj')[source]¶

Runs the entire simulation pipeline assuming certain data exists. :param config: Values from properly loaded configuration file. :param max_bins: The number of bins to process (useful if running tests). :param mag_table: Optional array of magnitudes. :param mag_table_keys Keys for optional mag table binning. :param provided_bgs: A set of provided background cutouts [OPTIONAL]. :param provided_psfs: A set of provided PSFs related to the provided backgrounds [OPTIONAL]. :param progress_bar: Have a TQDM progress bar. :param multiprocess_level: Where in the simulations to divide into cores

‘obj’ - Divide at the object level, where each core handles a single object in each bin. ‘bin’ - Divide at the bin level, so each core is responsible for a single bin ‘none’ - Do not do any multiprocessing at all (SLOW).

The object level is less memory intensive, but bins are processed one by one instead of simultaneously.

EXAMPLE USAGE:

config_values = tbridge.load_config_file(“path/to/config/file.tbridge”) tbridge.pipeline(config_values, max_bins=10)

Previous Next
